description.p: Tusk is a native PostgreSQL client for the GNOME desktop. It lets you browse schemas, inspect tables, run SQL queries, and export data — all from a clean, distraction-free interface that follows your system's dark and light mode.

ul.li: Browse schemas, tables, and views with a filterable sidebar
Inspect columns, keys, relations, indexes, triggers, and DDL
Paginated data tab with sortable columns and a quick filter bar
SQL editor with syntax highlighting, multi-statement execution, and auto-save
Run selected statement or the full buffer; cancel queries mid-execution
Copy or export results as CSV, JSON, or INSERT SQL
SSH tunnel support with key-based authentication
Passwords stored securely in GNOME Keyring

description.p: Connection dialog: moved the connection name field above the database settings for clearer layout.
Table inspector now shows estimated row count and total size on disk in a status bar at the bottom of the panel.
SQL editor: added a Cancel button to abort in-progress queries. Fixed overlapping query execution via keyboard shortcuts.
Data grid: sortable columns, client-side filter bar, and export to CSV, JSON, or INSERT SQL. Full-table export available from the table panel nav bar.
